title: God’s Knights Open the Abyss
description: On pages 7–9, this part focuses on Unknown person #7, Nami, Gunko, Unknown person #8, Luffy, Unknown person #9 as Luffy, Zoro, and Nami push toward freeing Loki while Scopper Gaban tests them and God’s Knights begin a darker move against Elbaf’s children.
scene: Luffy, Zoro, and Nami push toward freeing Loki while Scopper Gaban tests them and God’s Knights begin a darker move against Elbaf’s children.

dialogue.speaker: Gunko
dialogue.text: Let's start by taking their children hostage...

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: Shanks would never attack for no reason!!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: It's the truth. I saw him with my own eyes.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Red Hair has visited Elbaf many times... I recognized him!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: But I know shanks! He's a super cool guy! Maybe it was just someone who looked like him?

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: it was shocking; we were wondering what he was doing in the castle...

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Wouldn't it be weird for him to come right back just to skulk around?

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: He just left Elbaf recently, right?

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Even if you're telling the truth, there's no way it could be shanks!!

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: Shanks is always calm and laid-back!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: him doing something like this just doesn't add up!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: hehe... you really have faith in him, huh?

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: It'd tick you off just as much if someone badmouthed tell-mere!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #9
dialogue.text: So it was a look- alike?

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Shanks' brother??? Well, if he has one, he'd probably be a nice guy, too...

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: But honestly... he never talked about his family.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: I heard that he was raised on a pirate ship from when he was little...

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: I figure Roger and Rayleigh were his real family...

summary: This part advances the Scopper Gaban and Elbaf’s New Threat through 42 cleaned dialogue beats, emphasizing the core conflict, character stakes, and transition into the next movement of the story.
